name: apt
title: apt
description:
commandline package manager
This package provides commandline tools for searching and
managing as well as querying information about packages
as a low-level access to all features of the libapt-pkg library.
.
These include:
* apt-get for retrieval of packages and information about them
from authenticated sources and for installation, upgrade and
removal of packages together with their dependencies
* apt-cache for querying available information about installed
as well as installable packages
* apt-cdrom to use removable media as a source for packages
* apt-config as an interface to the configuration settings
* apt-extracttemplates is used by debconf to prompt for configuration
questions before installation.

commands.name: apt-mark
commands.description: show, set and unset various settings for a package

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use apt because commandline package manager.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use apt as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `apt  [-h]  [-o=config_string]  [-c=config_file]  [-t=target_release] [-a=architecture] {list |` as documented upstream for apt.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair apt with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `apt --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install apt. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `apt  [-h]  [-o=config_string]  [-c=config_file]  [-t=target_release] [-a=architecture] {list |`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: commandline package manager.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: commandline package manager.
